about.page.content.about.summary.label: Product overview
about.page.content.about.summary.title: A local-first offline media workspace
about.page.content.about.summary.descrip: Vidvix brings local media processing, AI-assisted workflows, and desktop utility features into one Windows interface so common tasks can stay as offline and direct as possible.
about.page.content.about.capabilities.de: The current experience is centered around these day-to-day media and desktop workflows:
about.page.content.about.capabilities.it: Video, audio, trim, merge, AI, split-audio, and terminal modules share output planning, progress feedback, and result reveal patterns.
The app hot-switches between Simplified Chinese and English, and supports system, light, and dark appearance preferences.
Bundled media and AI runtimes are maintained in-repo and preferred locally when available, reducing network dependency.
System tray behavior, window state persistence, shortcut creation, and copyable media details keep the workflow grounded in Windows desktop use.
about.page.content.about.openSource.desc: Some of Vidvix's core capabilities are made possible by these open-source projects. Click a project name to open its homepage or repository.
about.page.content.about.openSource.item: Handles media conversion, probing, track extraction, and the allowlisted command execution inside Terminal.
Powers preview and embedded playback surfaces for workflows that need visual feedback.
Drives offline four-stem source separation in the Split Audio workspace.
Provides the offline interpolation runtime behind the AI interpolation workflow.
Provides the offline super-resolution and enhancement runtime behind the AI enhancement workflow.
